Let (x∗, y∗) be a solution to any optimization problem max(x,y)∈R2 f(x, y) subject to
g1(x, y) ≤c1. Let (x′, y) be a solution to the same optimization problem max(x,y)∈R2 f(x, y)

the following statements is always true?

m > 0, p > 0 and y∗∈(0, m). Then which one of the following statements is true?
options: x∗depends on both p and m
x∗is independent of both p and m.

questions.question_text:
Let A and B be two events with positive probability each, deﬁned on the same sample
space. Find the correct answer:

Let A and B be two mutually exclusive events with positive probability each, deﬁned on
the same sample space. Find the correct answer:
options: A and B are necessarily independent
A and B are necessarily dependent
A and B are necessarily equally likely

questions.question_text:
The salaries of 16 players of a football club are given below (units are in thousands of
rupees).
100, 100, 111, 114, 165, 210, 225, 225, 230,
575, 1200, 1900, 2100, 2100, 2650, 3300
Now suppose each player received an extra Rs. 200,000 as bonus. Find the correct
answer:
options: Mean will increase by Rs. 200,000 but the median will not change
Both mean and median will be increased by Rs. 200,000
Mean and standard deviation will both be changed
Standard deviation will be increased but the median will be unchanged

the mean and the median:

Puja and Priya play a fair game (i.e.
winning probability is 1/2 for both players)
repeatedly for one rupee per game. If originally Puja has a rupees and Priya has b
rupees (where a > b), what is Puja’s chances of winning all of Priya’s money, assuming
the play goes on until one person has lost all her money?

An urn contains w white balls and b black balls (w > 0) and (b > 0).The balls are
thoroughly mixed and two are drawn, one after the other, without replacement. Let Wi
denote the outcome ’white on the i -th draw’ for i = 1, 2. Which one of the following is
true?

A bag contains four pieces of paper, each labeled with one of the digits 1, 2, 3, 4, with
no repeats. Three of these pieces are drawn, one at a time without replacement, to
construct a three-digit number. What is the probability that the three-digit number is
a multiple of 3?

Consider two random variables X and Y where X takes values -2,-1,0,1,2 each with
probability 1/5 and Y = |X|. Which of the following is true?
options:
The variables X and Yare independent and Pearson’s correlation coeﬃcient
between X and Y is 0.

The variables X and Y are dependent and Pearson’s correlation coeﬃcient
between X and Y is 0.

The variables X and Y are independent and Pearson’s correlation coeﬃcient
between X and Y is 1

The variables X and Y are dependent and Pearson’s correlation coeﬃcient
between X and Y is 1

questions.question_text:
Two friends who take the metro to their jobs from the same station arrive to the station
uniformly randomly between 7 and 7: 20 in the morning. They are willing to wait for
one another for 5 minutes, after which they take a train whether together or alone. What
is the probability of their meeting at the station?
